zh.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne於1203年出生在英國倫敦。她於大約1240年與Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar在英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯。

zh-hans.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: 英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne于1203年出生在英国倫敦。她于大约1240年与Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar在英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世在英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne was born in 1203, in London, England. She married Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar about 1240, in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne est née en 1203, à Londres, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar environ 1240, à Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée à Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne wurde 1203 in London, England geboren. Sie hat um 1240 in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne è nata il nel 1203 nel London, England. Lei sposato Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar circa 1240, nel Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England.

ja.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルス
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payneは1203年にイギリスロンドンで生まれた。約1240年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルスでSir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne(은)는 1203년 영국 런던에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1240년 영국 레이스스터실르 마크트 보스워스에서 Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 레이스스터실르 마크트 보스워스에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne nasceu em 1203, em London, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar aproximadamente 1240, em Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne родилась в 1203 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar около 1240 г. в местности маркет босворт, лисестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в местности маркет босворт, лисестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Payne nació en 1203, en London, Inglaterra. Se casó con Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar aproximadamente 1240, en Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
